name: Point To Point School Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Charter/rental clients in Dallas-Mesquite area (church groups, construction crew shuttles, camps, corporate/event charters); NOT a daily school district contractor for Mesquite ISD
recent_news: No recent news; FMCSA shows 1 truck/1 driver intrastate carrier with no inspections since 2019, suggesting micro-operator with minimal activity
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in fit. Despite the name, this is a 1-truck/1-driver charter-rental bus service (church/camp/corporate events), not a contracted school transportation provider. Employee count of 32 in source data appears inflated vs FMCSA filings. No district contracts, no scale, not a real student-transportation acquisition target.

name: School Bus Logistics LLC
principals: Robert (Rob) Jacobus (Founder; 2019 STN National Transportation Director of the Year)
districts_or_customers: School districts nationally (consulting clients, not transportation contracts); recently expanded staff into CO, NY, IL, TX
recent_news: 2024: enhanced RouteHub portal with behavior incident reporting; added four team members expanding presence in CO/NY/IL/TX; ~$1M revenue in 2024 per Crunchbase
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in fit. This is a pupil-transportation consulting and SaaS routing firm (RouteHub), not a bus operator. Owns no buses, employs no drivers. Founder Rob Jacobus is a well-known industry consultant. Could be a vendor/partner to Summit but not an acquisition target for a bus-operator roll-up.

name: Beal School Bus Co
principals: Denny Beal (owner per APS contractor list); John Beal (historical principal per Yelp listing)
districts_or_customers: Albuquerque Public Schools (APS) - listed as current active contractor 'Beal Bus Co.' operating bus numbers 0002-0026 (~25 buses); also listed on NM PED School Bus Contractor List
recent_news: No recent M&A or news; long-tenured APS contractor (60 years in business, FMCSA registration since 2009 under current DOT)
fit_notes: GOOD fit candidate. 60-year-old family-owned APS contractor with ~25 buses on the APS dedicated contractor list (one of only six). High succession signal given multi-generational name (John -> Denny Beal) and longevity. Albuquerque is an attractive Southwest tuck-in market with stable, multi-route APS revenue. Small size means it would be a bolt-on, but the embedded APS relationship is the strategic asset.

name: Consolidated Bus Resources
principals: None publicly identified (no lead_exec in source data)
service_types: unclear - possible school bus brokerage/consulting rather than operating
districts_or_customers: Not confirmed; no NJ school district contracts identified; not in publicly-cited NJ School Bus Contractors Association directory results
website: businessconnectorllc.com (this domain resolves to 'Business Resource Connector LLC,' a general small-business consulting firm in West Orange NJ run by Willie Tolbert - NOT a school bus operator)
fit_notes: LIKELY NOT a tuck-in fit. The associated website (businessconnectorllc.com) belongs to a general small-business consulting LLC, not a bus operator. 'Consolidated Bus Resources' does not surface as an NJ school bus contractor and may be a defunct entity, paper company, or mis-classified consulting/brokerage. Recommend disqualifying without further primary-source confirmation (NJ DOE contractor codes, Secretary of State filing).

name: Claxton Bus Lines Inc
principals: Privately held; specific owner not publicly disclosed in search results
districts_or_customers: Anderson County Schools (Tennessee) - long-tenured primary customer (40+ years); churches and private groups in the Powell/Knoxville/Oak Ridge region
recent_news: No M&A activity identified; described as 'the premier pupil transport service in the region' with on-site state-of-the-art maintenance facility; 13 TN-certified school buses
fit_notes: DECENT bolt-on candidate. 40+ year independent operator with sole/primary contract at Anderson County Schools TN (~13 buses) plus charter ancillary. Owns maintenance facility (asset value, operational tuck-in). Small scale but the embedded district relationship in greater Knoxville is the strategic value; succession risk is plausible given age of business though specific owner not named. Worth a direct outreach call.
